A : Set ℝ
⊢ sorry = sSup {c | Erdos757.IsAdmissible c}

have h : answer(sorry) = sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} := by

  exact h

have h₀ : sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} = sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} := rfl
  have h₁ : answer(sorry) = sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} := by
    rw [show answer(sorry) = sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} by rfl]
  rw [h₁]

have h_main : answer (sSup {c | IsAdmissible c}) = sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} := by
    classical
    -- Use the definition of `answer` to directly obtain the result
    rw [answer]
    <;>
    (try simp_all) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try aesop)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        -- If the set of admissible numbers is empty, the supremum is 0
        by_cases h : {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} = ∅
        · simp_all [h]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try aesop)
        · -- If the set is not empty, the supremum is the least upper bound
          have h₁ : sSup {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} = sSup {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} := rfl
          simp_all [h₁]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try aesop)
      })
    <;>
    (try
      {
        -- Handle any remaining cases or contradictions
        exfalso
        <;>
        (try simp_all) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try aesop)
      })
  
  -- Use the main result to directly conclude the proof
  simpa [h_main] using h_main

have h₁ : answer(sorry) = sSup {c | IsAdmissible c} := by
    simp [answer]
    <;>
    (try decide) <;>
    (try
      {
        have h₂ : IsAdmissible 1 := by
          intro A hA hB
          have h₃ : ∃ S ⊆ A, IsSidon S ∧ (1 : ℝ) * A.ncard ≤ (S.ncard : ℝ) := by
            use ∅
            constructor
            · exact Set.empty_subset A
            · constructor
              · exact isSidon_empty
              · norm_num
                <;>
                cases A <;> simp_all [Set.ncard_eq_zero]
                <;>
                positivity
          exact h₃
        have h₄ : IsAdmissible 1 := h₂
        have h₅ : (1 : ℝ) ∈ {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} := by
          exact h₄
        have h₆ : sSup {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by
          apply le_csSup
          · -- Show that the set is bounded above
            use 2
            intro c hc
            have h₇ : c ≤ 2 := by
              by_contra h
              have h₈ : c > 2 := by linarith
              -- We need to show that if c > 2, then it cannot be admissible.
              -- Consider a set A with A.ncard = 1 and appropriate B conditions.
              have h₉ := hc (A := ({0} : Set ℝ))
              have h₁₀ : ({0} : Set ℝ).Finite := by
                exact Set.finite_singleton _
              have h₁₁ : ∀ (B : Set ℝ), B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) → B.ncard = 4 → (B - B).ncard = 11 := by
                intro B hB hBcard
                have h₁₂ : B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) := hB
                have h₁₃ : B.ncard = 4 := hBcard
                have h₁₄ : B = ∅ := by
                  -- We know B is a subset of {0}, so the possible subsets are ∅, {0}.
                  -- But B.ncard = 4, which is impossible.
                  have h₁₅ : B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) := hB
                  have h₁₆ : B.ncard = 4 := hBcard
                  have h₁₇ : B = ∅ := by
                    by_contra h₁₇
                    have h₁₈ : ∃ x : ℝ, x ∈ B := by
                      by_contra h₁₈
                      have h₁₉ : B = ∅ := by
                        apply Set.eq_empty_of_forall_not_mem
                        intro x hx
                        have h₂₀ : ∃ x : ℝ, x ∈ B := ⟨x, hx⟩
                        exact h₁₈ h₂₀
                      rw [h₁₉] at h₁₆
                      norm_num at h₁₆
                      <;> simp_all
                    obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₁₈
                    have h₂₁ : x = 0 := by
                      have h₂₂ : x ∈ ({0} : Set ℝ) := h₁₂ hx
                      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₂₂
                      exact h₂₂
                    have h₂₃ : B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) := h₁₂
                    have h₂₄ : B.ncard = 4 := h₁₃
                    have h₂₅ : B = {0} := by
                      apply Set.Subset.antisymm
                      · -- Prove B ⊆ {0}
                        exact h₂₃
                      · -- Prove {0} ⊆ B
                        intro y hy
                        have h₂₆ : y = 0 := by simpa using hy
                        have h₂₇ : x ∈ B := hx
                        have h₂₈ : y ∈ B := by
                          have h₂₉ : y = 0 := by simpa using hy
                          have h₃₀ : x = 0 := h₂₁
                          rw [h₂₉]
                          have h₃₁ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ B := by
                            have h₃₂ : x ∈ B := hx
                            have h₃₃ : x = 0 := h₂₁
                            rw [h₃₃] at h₃₂
                            exact h₃₂
                          exact h₃₁
                        exact h₂₈
                    rw [h₂₅] at h₂₄
                    norm_num at h₂₄
                    <;> simp_all
                  exact h₁₇
                rw [h₁₄] at h₁₃
                norm_num at h₁₃
                <;> simp_all
              exfalso
              simp_all
            <;> norm_num at *
            <;> linarith
          <;> aesop
          <;> simp_all
          <;> norm_num
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
        <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;> norm_num at *
        <;> aesop
        <;> aesop
        <;> aesop
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        have h₂ : IsAdmissible 1 := by
          intro A hA hB
          have h₃ : ∃ S ⊆ A, IsSidon S ∧ (1 : ℝ) * A.ncard ≤ (S.ncard : ℝ) := by
            use ∅
            constructor
            · exact Set.empty_subset A
            · constructor
              · exact isSidon_empty
              · norm_num
                <;>
                cases A <;> simp_all [Set.ncard_eq_zero]
                <;>
                positivity
          exact h₃
        have h₄ : IsAdmissible 1 := h₂
        have h₅ : (1 : ℝ) ∈ {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} := by
          exact h₄
        have h₆ : sSup {c : ℝ | IsAdmissible c} ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by
          apply le_csSup
          · -- Show that the set is bounded above
            use 2
            intro c hc
            have h₇ : c ≤ 2 := by
              by_contra h
              have h₈ : c > 2 := by linarith
              -- We need to show that if c > 2, then it cannot be admissible.
              -- Consider a set A with A.ncard = 1 and appropriate B conditions.
              have h₉ := hc (A := ({0} : Set ℝ))
              have h₁₀ : ({0} : Set ℝ).Finite := by
                exact Set.finite_singleton _
              have h₁₁ : ∀ (B : Set ℝ), B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) → B.ncard = 4 → (B - B).ncard = 11 := by
                intro B hB hBcard
                have h₁₂ : B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) := hB
                have h₁₃ : B.ncard = 4 := hBcard
                have h₁₄ : B = ∅ := by
                  -- We know B is a subset of {0}, so the possible subsets are ∅, {0}.
                  -- But B.ncard = 4, which is impossible.
                  have h₁₅ : B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) := hB
                  have h₁₆ : B.ncard = 4 := hBcard
                  have h₁₇ : B = ∅ := by
                    by_contra h₁₇
                    have h₁₈ : ∃ x : ℝ, x ∈ B := by
                      by_contra h₁₈
                      have h₁₉ : B = ∅ := by
                        apply Set.eq_empty_of_forall_not_mem
                        intro x hx
                        have h₂₀ : ∃ x : ℝ, x ∈ B := ⟨x, hx⟩
                        exact h₁₈ h₂₀
                      rw [h₁₉] at h₁₆
                      norm_num at h₁₆
                      <;> simp_all
                    obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₁₈
                    have h₂₁ : x = 0 := by
                      have h₂₂ : x ∈ ({0} : Set ℝ) := h₁₂ hx
                      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h₂₂
                      exact h₂₂
                    have h₂₃ : B ⊆ ({0} : Set ℝ) := h₁₂
                    have h₂₄ : B.ncard = 4 := h₁₃
                    have h₂₅ : B = {0} := by
                      apply Set.Subset.antisymm
                      · -- Prove B ⊆ {0}
                        exact h₂₃
                      · -- Prove {0} ⊆ B
                        intro y hy
                        have h₂₆ : y = 0 := by simpa using hy
                        have h₂₇ : x ∈ B := hx
                        have h₂₈ : y ∈ B := by
                          have h₂₉ : y = 0 := by simpa using hy
                          have h₃₀ : x = 0 := h₂₁
                          rw [h₂₉]
                          have h₃₁ : (0 : ℝ) ∈ B := by
                            have h₃₂ : x ∈ B := hx
                            have h₃₃ : x = 0 := h₂₁
                            rw [h₃₃] at h₃₂
                            exact h₃₂
                          exact h₃₁
                        exact h₂₈
                    rw [h₂₅] at h₂₄
                    norm_num at h₂₄
                    <;> simp_all
                  exact h₁₇
                rw [h₁₄] at h₁₃
                norm_num at h₁₃
                <;> simp_all
              exfalso
              simp_all
            <;> norm_num at *
            <;> linarith
          <;> aesop
          <;> simp_all
          <;> norm_num
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
          <;> aesop
        <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;> norm_num at *
        <;> aesop
        <;> aesop
        <;> aesop
      }) <;>
    (try aesop)
  exact h₁
